title: Windows installer: add hooks/ to sparse-checkout so .bee/bin/hooks/ gets vendored
action: install.ps1:136 sparse-checkout set omits hooks/, so onboard_bee.mjs listPluginHooks() sees an absent PLUGIN_HOOKS_DIR and returns [], vendoring zero hooks into .bee/bin/hooks/, while renderCodexHookEntries() is hardcoded so .codex/hooks.json is still written full -> every Codex hook MODULE_NOT_FOUND on Windows. Add hooks to the sparse set; add a static regression assertion in test_installers_e2e.mjs asserting the sparse set includes every tree onboarding reads.

trace.verification_evidence: {"red_failure_evidence":"Before fix: scripts/install.ps1:136 sparse-checkout set omitted hooks/. onboard_bee.mjs listPluginHooks() found PLUGIN_HOOKS_DIR (<clone>/hooks) absent, returned [], so the codexHybrid copy loop vendored ZERO hooks into .bee/bin/hooks/; renderCodexHookEntries() is hardcoded so .codex/hooks.json was still written full pointing at .bee/bin/hooks/bee-*.mjs. Reproduced on the users Windows install: .bee/bin/ held only bee.mjs+lib/ (no hooks/ subdir) -> every Codex hook MODULE_NOT_FOUND. install.sh full-clone was unaffected, so the bash-only e2e never caught it.","green_evidence":"After fix: hooks added to sparse set -> fetches <clone>/hooks -> listPluginHooks() returns 10 hooks -> .bee/bin/hooks/ vendored on Windows. New static regression check asserts sparse set includes skills,hooks,.claude-plugin,.codex-plugin,docs/history/codex-harness-hardening. Orchestrator verify re-run: test_installers_e2e (bash) 22 passed 0 failed exit 0."}
trace.verify_output: test_installers_e2e (bash): 22 passed, 0 failed; EXIT:0 (orchestrator independent re-run). New regression check 'install.ps1 sparse-checkout set fetches every tree onboarding reads (incl. hooks)' PASS.
